$0062: incremented at $c106 in the NMI tail; Main_Loop at $c2e8 spins until it reaches 3, then resets it. Effectively gates the game to one logic step per 3 VBlanks (~20 Hz)
$0063: NMI prologue stash for the A register (restored by the NMI epilogue before RTI)
$0065: NMI prologue stash for the Y register
$0087: byte count consumed by PPU_WriteBlock ($801b); 58 write sites across PPU-update code
$0098: P1 controller raw state this frame (8 bits) populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=0
$0099: P1 controller newly-pressed bits this frame (rising edges of Joypad1_State)
$009a: P2 controller raw state this frame (populated by Joypad_PollOne for X=1)
$009b: P2 controller newly-pressed bits this frame
$0088: low byte of PPU destination address ($2006 target) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$0089: high byte of PPU destination address; $89:$88 is the full $2006 word
$008a: low byte of source pointer read by PPU_WriteBlock at STA $2007; zero-page ($8a),Y fetch
$008b: high byte of source pointer ($8b:$8a) used by PPU_WriteBlock
$008c: mirror of last value written to $2000; flipping bit $80 gates NMI and is reapplied across routines
$048e: entity SOA attribute 0 (active flag): bit $80 = slot in use, tested by L_f7cd. The whole entity table is 25 attributes x 25 slots, $19-byte stride per attribute, running $048e..$0682. Slot 0 is the player; Reset_Rolls clears slots 0-11 (player + co-active aux entities)
$04a7: entity SOA attribute 1 (vertical position); 25 slots at +0..+24 within the table. Slot 0 is the player: UP drives toward $56 (top), DOWN toward $c1 (bottom)
$04c0: entity SOA attribute 2 (horizontal position); 25 slots. Slot 0 is the player: LEFT drives toward $1e (left edge), RIGHT toward $e6 (right edge). L_aa61 reads Entity_X,X / Entity_X,Y for collision checks between two arbitrary slots
$03b2: shadow-OAM mirror of Entity_X[0] (player), written via DMA
$00db: frames the B (Fire) button has been held; Fire_Handler (L_d2aa) increments while held and rolls over at 10 to call Spawn_Bullet — that's the auto-fire repeat rate (~10 frames between bullets when B is held)
$0388: per-button 'held last frame' flags consumed by the input dispatchers. bit $10 = B (Fire), set/cleared by Fire_Handler ($d2aa); bit $01 = A (Roll), set/cleared by Roll_Handler at $d214. Each handler does a rising-edge detect against its own bit
$0785: current-frame joypad state stored MSB-first (A=$80, B=$40, SEL=$20, START=$10, UP=$08, DOWN=$04, LEFT=$02, RIGHT=$01). NOTE: bit ordering is the OPPOSITE of $98/$9a (which are LSB-first from Joypad_PollOne) — this byte is filled elsewhere in the input stack. Fire_Handler tests bit $40 (B); the input handlers at L_d472 / L_dc54 consume this same byte
$0386: 0 = P1 currently playing, 1 = P2 currently playing. NewGame_Init_P1 inits to 0; SwapTo_P2 sets to 1, SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave set to 0
$0427: start of the 33-byte ($21) block holding the currently playing player's state. First 5 bytes ($0427..$042b) are the HUD-display score (BCD digits, low-to-high) — Game Genie 0427:00..042B:00 collectively zero the visible score. Block also holds Lives ($0432), Rolls ($0436), Level, etc.; swapped via SwapTo_P1 / SwapTo_P2 in 2P games. The canonical (true) score lives separately at TrueScore_Lo ($07bf)
$044a: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P1 state while P2 is playing. SwapTo_P2 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P1 / Restore_P1_NoSave load it back into ActivePlayer_State
$046b: 33-byte ($21) save buffer for P2 state while P1 is playing. SwapTo_P1 saves the active block here; SwapTo_P2 loads it back into ActivePlayer_State
$03ae: nonzero -> NMI_Handler walks the PPU_UpdateQueue. NMI clears it on completion ($c07e); set by the queue builder at $81e2
$078f: variable-length PPU update queue consumed by NMI_Handler. Each entry: addr_hi, addr_lo, count, count×data_byte; queue terminates when count == 0
$07bd: first half of the warm-boot magic word; Reset_Handler skips RAM-clear when $07bd:$07be == $55:$aa
$07be: second half of the warm-boot magic word; together with WarmBoot_Magic1 distinguishes warm restart from cold power-on
$07bc: 0 = 1P game, nonzero = 2P; set in L_b6da/L_b6f7 from menu choice, gates 2P paths in Continue_Init_P1 / NewGame_Init_P2
$0385: $ff while P1 is the active player; cleared on game-over. Set at $b7da during NewGame_Init_P1
$0384: companion to P1_Active; tested by P1_LifeLost at $b80a to decide whether to swap players or game-over
$801b: copy PPU_WriteCount bytes from ($PPU_Src),Y to $2007, starting at $2006 = PPU_AddrHi:PPU_AddrLo. Called 25x; the main PPU-update primitive
$c1ad: strobe both joypads ($4016=1 then 0), then call Joypad_PollOne for X=0 (P1) and X=1 (P2). Called once per NMI from $c103 (just before VBlank_Counter is incremented)
$c1c7: shift 8 bits out of $4016,X with delta-detect: writes raw state to $98+X*2 and newly-pressed bits to $99+X*2. Bit order matches the standard A/B/SEL/START/UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT layout
$bf58: swap active player P1 -> P2: copy ActivePlayer_State to P1_SavedState, then load P2_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 1
$bf74: swap active player P2 -> P1: copy ActivePlayer_State to P2_SavedState, then load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State, set Active_Player_Index = 0
$bf7f: alternate entry into SwapTo_P1: skip the save half and only load P1_SavedState into ActivePlayer_State. Used by Continue_Init_P1 because the dying P1 state is being discarded
$bf90: RESET / IRQ vector entry: SEI + CLD, set stack to $f3, enable NMI bit, wait for VBlank, then check WarmBoot_Magic. Cold path zeros all of $0000-$07ff and writes the magic; warm path skips the wipe. Both paths fall into Init_PPU_And_MainLoop
$bfca: post-Reset init: clear zero page, configure PPU ($2001 sprites on, $2000 NMI+8x16), clear scroll, fill OAM with $ff, enable APU, set up nametable pointer at $d0/$d1. Falls through to Main_Loop
$c2c0: cooperative-multitasking fiber scheduler. Loads scan position from Coroutine_ScanPos ($00), then walks the 16 fiber slots ($02,X for X=$00 to $1e step 2) looking for one with ready bit ($80) set. On match, jumps to Coroutine_Resume ($c2f5) which restores that fiber's SP, X, Y, A and `pla / plp / rts` returns into the fiber's saved code. If scan reaches slot $20 (end), waits for VBlank_Counter >= 3, resets it, re-asserts NMI enable, resets scan position to 0, and restarts the scan. Game logic runs entirely as fibers that yield via Coroutine_Yield ($c275)
$c030: NMI vector entry: stash A/X/Y to $63-$65, disable NMI bit in $2000, OAM-DMA the shadow OAM at $0200 via $4014, blank rendering, then if PPU_QueueReady drain PPU_UpdateQueue into $2006/$2007 and clear the flag
$a9ce: trivial SEC; RTS helper used 8x by callers that want to propagate 'failed/not-found' via carry
$b7d8: initialize P1 state at game start: clears player flags, sets Lives_Ones = $02 at $b7df (Game Genie IESUTYZA patches the immediate at $b7de). Falls through into the P1 input/play loop
$b807: P1 life-loss handler: JSR Death_Sequence, then dec Lives_Ones; on underflow borrow from Lives_Tens (set ones to 9) and re-enter at $b807. Game-over when both digits are zero
$b842: P1 continue logic in 2P mode: if TwoPlayer_Mode != 0, JSR $bf7f then reset Lives_Ones = $02 at $b84a (Game Genie IAKUUAZA patches the immediate at $b84b)
$b858: initialize P2 lives: LDA #$02 at $b858 / STA Lives_Ones at $b85a (Game Genie IASUOAZA patches the immediate at $b859). Followed by clearing the 18-byte P2 score+lives buffer at $0437..$0448
$b889: P2 life-loss handler: parallel to P1_LifeLost; same dec-with-tens-borrow pattern, with the borrow restoring Lives_Ones to $09
$d7a1: called from every life-loss path (P1_LifeLost, P2_LifeLost, etc.); sets GameState_Active ($07c5) and $0718 to $ff and clears $0439-$043c, then waits/clears state before the player respawns. Note: GameState_Active is also touched in init ($c02a→0) and game-over ($e195→0), so $ff here marks 'session in progress', not a death-only flag
$d850: set Rolls = $03 at $d852 (Game Genie PASIOALE patches the immediate at $d851), then clear $048e..$049b (12-byte block, probably entity slot)
$d2aa: B (Fire) button handler: tests bit $40 of Buttons_Held; on first press OR every 10 frames of holding, JSR Spawn_Bullet ($d3b9). Uses Fire_DebounceFlag to detect rising edge and Fire_HoldCounter to time the auto-fire repeat. Confirmed by autofire scenario: holding B causes Spawn_Bullet to populate slot 11
$d3b9: spawn a player bullet: stores entity-slot index $0b at $071a and $01 at $0719, then sets Entity_Active[$0b] = $88 (active+sprite-flag). Slot 11 is the player bullet slot. Confirmed live: pressing B activates slot 11 in the entity SOA
$0719: $01 written by Spawn_Bullet alongside Bullet_TargetSlot — likely a one-shot 'spawn requested' flag consumed by the entity update pass
$071a: $0b written by Spawn_Bullet — the entity slot index ($0b = 11) where the new bullet is being installed. Read at L_d3c3 to look up Entity_Active,Y
$0437: INC at L_e1e0 each time the player respawns; cleared to 0 by NewGame_Init paths and by debe (post-game-over re-init). Confirmed live: across a 5-death autofire run this byte went 0,0,1,1,2,3,3,4,5 (one increment per death). Tested at >10 sites as a 'have we died yet?' flag (e.g. d09a, d68b — those branches just want nonzero, not the count)
$d214: A (Roll/Loop) button handler. Sits in a .byte block the disassembler couldn't reach (entered via jmp $d214 at $d1dc). Pre-checks via jsr $d79b / jsr $b3cf (likely 'is player alive and idle'), then tests bit $80 of Buttons_Held; on rising edge sets bit $01 of Button_DebounceFlags and JSR Roll_Attempt ($d303). Falls through into Fire_Handler ($d248) so both buttons get serviced each frame. Confirmed live: press_a scenario decrements Rolls 03→02 and sets Entity_Active[0] bit 4
$d303: if Rolls == 0 RTS, else DEC Rolls. Three-instruction routine sitting in a .byte block ($ad $36 $04 $d0 $01 $60 $ce $36 $04). Called from Roll_Handler ($d23a) on the rising edge of A. This is the gate that makes the A button consume one roll
$d472: D-pad input handler — sits in a .byte block. Reads Buttons_Held, masks the low 4 bits (RIGHT=$01, LEFT=$02, DOWN=$04, UP=$08) and dispatches to one of 8 per-direction movement routines. Each per-direction routine calls SetEntityDirection ($d593) with a 4-bit code, bounds-checks Entity_X[X] / Entity_Y[X] against the screen edges, then either JMPs ApplyMovement ($d589) or PlayerMove_NoOp ($d592). Direction codes: $00=UP, $02=UP+RIGHT, $04=RIGHT, $06=DOWN+RIGHT, $08=DOWN, $0a=DOWN+LEFT, $0c=LEFT, $0e=UP+LEFT. Confirmed live: move_left scenario sets Entity_Direction = $0c and drops Entity_X to $1e (left edge clamp)
$d589: do the per-frame X/Y update for entity X after the dpad handlers commit a direction. Loads entity SOA attribute 12 ($05ba,X), calls $b048 (sprite update) and $b1d7. Reached only via JMP from the d472 dispatch arms (every direction handler ends in `jmp ApplyMovement` once its bounds checks pass)
$d592: single-byte RTS reached when the dpad input would push the entity off the screen edge. Each direction arm of DPad_Handler jumps here instead of ApplyMovement when the bounds check fails
$d593: if A == Entity_Direction[X], early-return (no change). Otherwise save the old direction to PrevDirection_Save ($048d) and store the new value in Entity_Direction[X] ($050b,X). Called by every arm of DPad_Handler before the move is applied
$050b: entity SOA attribute 5 (8-direction code, $0..$e in steps of 2): $00=UP, $02=UP+RIGHT, $04=RIGHT, $06=DOWN+RIGHT, $08=DOWN, $0a=DOWN+LEFT, $0c=LEFT, $0e=UP+LEFT. Set by SetEntityDirection from the dpad handler. Confirmed live: move_left puts $0c into $050b
$048d: one-frame stash of the previous Entity_Direction. Written by SetEntityDirection ($d593) before it overwrites $050b,X — lets downstream code detect a direction change
$008f: X-scroll shadow written to PPUSCROLL at $c0e7 each NMI tail. Sourced at $80b3 from $039f (mirror in 33-byte player state); also zeroed by level-reset paths at $bcf9 and $bfea
$0090: Y-scroll shadow written to PPUSCROLL at $c0ec each NMI tail (the second of the two PPUSCROLL writes that latch X then Y). Sourced at $80b3 from $03a0; cleared by level-reset paths at $bcfb and $bfef. Static during long_idle scenario — the auto-scroll only advances once gameplay action begins
$03a0: persistent Y-scroll source in the active player state block. Copied into $0090 each NMI tail at $80b3, then latched into PPUSCROLL
$03a1: low bit drives PPUCTRL bit 1 (nametable X) at $80b8 — the horizontal scroll-wrap selector. ASL/AND #$02 pattern means bit 0 of this byte toggles which nametable is mapped
$0725: frames-of-current-second for the HUD blink driver: incremented every NMI at $c136 (after the entity-decay loop and SP restore), reset to 0 when it hits $3c (60). Only ticks when P1_Active is set
$0726: seconds counter for the HUD blink driver: incremented at $c145 each time HUD_BlinkSubcount wraps. Bit 0 selects between two 12-byte HUD glyph blocks ($c372 vs $c366) that get copied to OAM at $0200, producing the 1Hz on/off blink (PUSH START / 1UP-style indicator). Confirmed by long_idle drift: +1 per ~1 sec of in-game time
$071f: 8-state rotating index used by the enemy-spawn routine. Read at $f054 / $f176 / $f34c / $f42c (each masks `and #$07`, ASLs twice to get a 4-byte stride, indexes the spawn-parameter table at $f12e to fetch four per-spawn fields), then incremented in place. Drives wave-pattern variety
$043d: low byte of a 16-bit running tally incremented at $ee67 (right after the entity-spawn site that does `lda #$88 / sta Entity_Active,X`). Carries into Spawn_Tally_Hi via `bne / inc $043e`. Drift confirms ~1 increment per spawn during idle gameplay
$043e: high byte of the 16-bit spawn tally; incremented at $ee6c when Spawn_Tally_Lo wraps
$0439: low byte of a parallel 16-bit tally incremented at $ee5f in the same code as Spawn_Tally_Lo (two counters bumped together at every spawn). Likely a per-life or per-stage spawn count vs. the $043d running total
$043a: high byte of the parallel spawn tally; incremented at $ee64 when Spawn_TallyAlt_Lo wraps
$022d: loop/dive counter for the player. Game Genie 022D:03 (Infinite Dives) pins this to $03, so the player can always loop. Companion to Rolls ($0436) — the two together are 1942's twin defensive moves
$07bf: lowest BCD digit of the actual (canonical) score. Game Genie 07BF:00 forces this to 0 every frame. The full true-score block is 6 BCD bytes at $07bf..$07c4, written by the score-add routines and copied to the HUD-display copy at $0427..$042b. Pinning all 6 to 0 (the 'True Score' cheat) makes the player rank as 0 score for high-score purposes
$07c0: second BCD digit of the true score (see TrueScore_Lo for full block notes)
$07c1: third BCD digit of the true score
$07c2: fourth BCD digit of the true score
$07c3: fifth BCD digit of the true score
$07c4: highest BCD digit of the true score
$b824: `dec Lives_Ones` instruction targeted by Game Genie SZXLKEVK (Infinite Lives P1). Sits in a .byte block ($ce $32 $04 $4c $07 $b8 = `dec Lives_Ones / jmp P1_LifeLost`); the cheat overwrites the $ce opcode with $ad (lda) so the decrement becomes a harmless load. Reached from the borrow path that ran when Lives_Ones underflowed and the tens-borrow restored ones to 9
$b44b: `jsr L_aa61` (player-vs-enemy collision check) targeted by Game Genie SZKULGAX (Invincibility). Patching the $20 opcode to $ad turns the JSR into a no-op load, so the collision routine is never entered. Companion code ALKUTGEL flips the following BCS at $b44e to BMI to keep the surrounding flow correct
$b56b: `and #$10` test of Entity_Active bit 4 inside the player-collision walker (Y=0..2). Game Genie OZVULSPX (Hit Anywhere / no-collision) overwrites the $29 opcode with $a9 (lda) so the AND becomes a load — the BNE following always sees nonzero and the JSR L_aa61 collision check is skipped
$f813: sister of $b56b in the broader entity-vs-entity collision pass: `and #$10` on Entity_Active,Y. Game Genie OZOYUEPX patches the $29 to $a9 (lda), short-circuiting the same collision check across more slots than OZVULSPX covers
$d309: `dec Rolls` instruction inside Roll_Attempt (.byte block). Game Genie SZESPUVK (Infinite Rolls) patches the $ce opcode at $d309 with $ad (lda), turning the decrement into a no-op. Confirms Roll_Attempt at $d303 spans through $d30b
$d2bc: immediate operand of `ora #$10` inside Fire_Handler at $d2bb that sets bit 4 of Button_DebounceFlags. Game Genie AEUSGZAP (Rapid Fire P1) patches the $10 to $00 so the debounce bit is never set — every frame counts as a fresh button-press, producing one bullet spawn per frame instead of one per 10 frames
$dc01: `lda #$ff / sta $0718` at the entry of one of the death/respawn handlers; sets the death-state byte $0718 to $ff. Game Genie PAEIXKNY patches the $ff immediate at $dc02 to $01, changing the value stored — likely the 'Easier Difficulty' cheat that downgrades the post-death state
$f828: `sta Entity_Active,Y` after `ora #$01` (sets bit 0 on the slot's active flag — likely the 'enemy is hit/dying' bit). Game Genie OLZNEE is a 6-letter unconditional poke that overwrites the $99 opcode with $b9 (lda), suppressing the store — enemies never have this bit set, which would change hit/death animation flow
$042e: set to $ff at $ac7e once the score crosses an extra-life threshold; a nonzero value suppresses re-checking the threshold (the bne $ac8e bypass at $ac72) so each crossing only awards one life. Cleared on game-init
$042f: high BCD digit of the running 'next extra-life' threshold. Bumped at $acbd via `lda $042f / clc / adc #$01` after each extra life is awarded — combined with the +8 carry into ExtraLife_NextThresh_Lo this raises the next threshold by 80,000 points
$0430: low BCD digit of the next-extra-life threshold; cleared by the threshold trigger at $ac85 then BCD-incremented by 8 at $acad each award (40 -> 48 wraps to 50, etc.). The first threshold (Score_D4 >= 2 -> 200,000) is hardcoded at $ac74 — these counters only steer subsequent extra lives
$ac1a: add to the player's BCD score and refresh the HUD. Inputs: $8a/$8b/$88/$89 hold the per-digit deltas to add into Score_D1/D2/D3/D4 (each 0..9). Score_D5 ($0427 / ActivePlayer_State) just propagates carry. Saves X/Y to $038b/$038c (which are otherwise general scratch), runs a 5-stage `cmp #$0a / sbc #$0a` BCD ripple across the digits, then JSRs HUD_Score_To_OAM at $ad39 and falls through into the extra-life check at $ac6f. All known callers set $8a/$8b/$88/$89 directly with hardcoded constants, e.g. $f8d3 sets $8b=$01 (score +100), $f939 sets $8a=$05 (score +5)
$ac14: alt entry into Score_AddBCD that first zeros $88:$89 — call this when the score increment fits in two BCD digits (caller only sets $8a:$8b). Used by $d7df and $f947 (in .byte block)
$ac6f: fall-through tail of Score_AddBCD: if ExtraLife_PendingFlag is already set, skip; else if Score_D4 (10,000s) >= 2 (i.e. score crossed 200,000) award an extra life and arm the next threshold counter; finally drop into Score_Sync ($acc8). Reachable both via fallthrough and standalone call sites that just want the threshold/sync without an add
$acc8: compare TrueScore_Lo..Hi ($07bf..$07c4) digit-by-digit MSB-first against ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 ($0427..$042b). If True < Display anywhere along the walk: branch to $ace0 (copy Display->True), then reload True from the per-player save buffer ($acf2) so a swap-in P2 doesn't clobber the unsaved P1 score. Returns by restoring caller's X/Y from $038b/$038c
$ace0: 6-byte copy loop: ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 -> TrueScore_Lo..Hi. Reached from Score_Sync when the display has caught up past the saved true value
$acf2: after Score_CopyDispToTrue, reload TrueScore from P1_SavedState (Active_Player_Index = 0) or P2_SavedState (= 1). Means TrueScore is the *common-bus* score working register and the per-player save buffer holds the *durable* digits across player swaps
$ad0e: INC Lives_Ones; if it hits $0a wrap to 0 and INC Lives_Tens (capping at 9 to give the 99-life ceiling). Calls PPU_Queue_Refresh twice with $10 then $11 — the two HUD-refresh slots that redraw the lives digits
$ad39: copy the 6 BCD score digits into the score sprites at $020c. First syncs ActivePlayer_State..Score_D1 (low nibble masked) into the active player's save buffer ($044a P1 or $046b P2), then blasts a 24-byte sprite block from a per-player template ($ad99 for P1, $adb1 for P2) into OAM, and finally re-patches the 6 tile-id slots from the just-synced digits. Called from the tail of Score_AddBCD
$a06b: HUD/PPU tile-refresh dispatcher. Caller passes a 5-bit refresh-target id in A; the routine derives a PPU_WriteCount and PPU source pointer from a base table at $a08c indexed by (A & 7), enqueues the redraw via PPU_WriteBlock infrastructure. ~10+ call sites across HUD-update paths (lives, score, level indicators)
$abc2: turn A (hitbox-class index 0..15) into a pointer at $abd0 + idx*4 stored in $8a:$8b. Each entry is a 4-byte (left, right, top, bottom) collision-box extent. Tail-called from the per-sprite-tile dispatch at Hitbox_Switch ($ab2c). Note: previously mislabeled as Score_LookupValue — the giveaway was the AABB-overlap math at $aaa3-$ab1e in the caller (Collision_AABB at $aa61)
$b6c5: title-screen menu input handler. Tests Joypad1_State bit 5 ($20 = SELECT) for cursor cycle, then bit 4 ($10 = START) below at $b731 to commit the choice. SELECT cycles MenuCursor_Index 0->1->2->(3 wraps to 0). Sits in a chain of paths via .byte blocks; the START path at $b75e reads the cursor and jumps into game-init
$07bb: title-screen menu cursor (0=1P normal, 1=1P alt, 2=2P, masked to 0..3). Incremented on each SELECT rising edge at $b6dd; the value drives MenuCursor_Y at $04b2 and TwoPlayer_Mode at $07bc. When the player commits with START, $b771 copies this byte to $0381 as the chosen-mode latch
$0382: title-screen SELECT-held debounce flag: nonzero means SELECT was held last frame. Set to $ff at $b6d5 on the rising edge so the cursor only advances once per press; cleared at $b717 when SELECT is released
$04b2: Y position of the title-screen menu cursor — physically slot 11 of Entity_Y (the bullet slot during gameplay), repurposed on the title screen. Set to $7f / $8f / $9f for the three menu rows by the dispatch arms at $b6fc / $b702 / $b70a
$0381: mode chosen on the title screen, latched in by START at $b771 via `lda MenuCursor_Index / sta $0381`. Read by the game-init paths to pick the 1P/2P scenario
$07c5: game-state flag: $00 at boot/title and after game-over (cleared at $c02a in init and $e195 in game-over alongside Lives_Tens/Ones); $ff during a life via Death_Sequence at $d7a3. Two title-screen consumers gate on it being nonzero: $b6f0 (skip the 2P menu row when cleared) and $bb1e (pick alternate HUD-tile sequence). Most consistent reading is 'a game session is in progress / has occurred since boot'
$070b: title-screen frame counter for the menu-cursor blink: initialized to $ff at $b6c0 just before the menu loop entry, decremented every iteration at $b744. When (timer & 3)==0 the loop falls through to the cursor-blink eor at $b74e-b75a (the 5-byte mask at TitleMenu_CursorMask). Confirmed via idle_titlescreen drift: SAW with constant -50 step over 150-frame chunks (consistent with one dec per frame, modulo wrap)
$032a: 5 bytes ($032a-$032e) holding the title-menu cursor sprite mask. The blink loop at $b74e-b75a does `ldy #$04 / lda $032a,Y / eor #$ff / sta $032a,Y / dey / bpl` every (TitleMenu_FrameTimer & 3)==0, so the mask flips between two states roughly every 4 frames. Confirmed via idle_titlescreen drift: all 5 bytes FLIP between $00 and $ff in lockstep
$00d4: low byte of a 16-bit pointer ($d4:$d5) into the active stage's enemy-spawn script. Initialized at $d97b from StageSpawnPtr_Table[NametableSelect>>1]. Advanced by 3 each spawn record at $d9a4 / $da76 (matches the 3-byte spawn-record stride elsewhere). Confirmed via die_once drift: monotonic UP across an in-game run, walking through the stage script
$00d5: high byte of the 16-bit spawn-script pointer; carries the +0 add when SpawnTable_PtrLo overflows at $d9aa / $da7c
$e26f: table of 16-bit little-endian pointers, indexed by NametableSelect>>1, that locate the start of each stage's enemy-spawn script. Read at $d978/$d97e (and again at $dc3c/$dc4c) to seed SpawnTable_PtrLo/Hi when a stage begins
$e170: reached by fall-through from $e16d after several teardown JSRs (e240, bcf1) when NametableSelect==0 at $e14a. Loads the 14-byte text from StageEnd_TextTiles ($e19e) and queues it via PPU_PrintTextBlock to PPU $21c9, then zeros Lives_Tens, Lives_Ones, GameState_Active, before falling into `jmp L_e1e0` (which inc's Respawn_Count). Likely the stage-clear / 'CONGRATULATION' transition rather than pure game-over, given the Respawn_Count bump and that game-over normally enters via P1_LifeLost when Lives underflows. Hedged: the Lives/GameState_Active zeroing makes it ambiguous without per-frame tracing
$e19e: 14 BG tile-id bytes consumed by StageEnd_Sequence at $e170: $0c $18 $17 $10 $1b $0a $1d $1e $15 $0a $1d $12 $18 $17. Decoded with the standard A=10..Z=35 mapping this spells 'CONGRATULATION' (no terminating S — likely fits the 14-tile slot). Confirms StageEnd_Sequence is the stage-clear text path
$0524: entity SOA attribute 6: high bit of the 24-bit fixed-point Y position. Updated in the Y-position integration at $b061-b08c where the carry from `lda $0399 / adc $0524,X` propagates back via `and #$01 / sta $0524,X`. Slot 0 = player. Pairs with Entity_PosY_FracMid ($053d, attr 7) and Entity_PosY_FracLo ($0556, attr 8) and the displayed integer Entity_Y ($04a7, attr 1)
$053d: entity SOA attribute 7: middle byte of the 24-bit Y position. The physics-tick at $b055-b05b reads `$039a` (Y-velocity mid) and adds it: `lda $039a / adc $053d,X / sta $053d,X`. Carries up into Entity_PosY_HiBit. Confirmed live: copied during slot-clone at $b1ab
$0556: entity SOA attribute 8: lowest sub-pixel byte of the Y position. The physics-tick at $b04c-b052 adds `$039b` (Y-velocity low) into it: `lda $039b / adc $0556,X / sta $0556,X`. Carries into Entity_PosY_FracMid. Confirmed live: copied during slot-clone at $b1a5
$056f: entity SOA attribute 9: high bit of the 24-bit X position. Updated at $b0a2-b0aa where `lda $0396 / adc $056f,X / and #$01 / sta $056f,X` propagates the X-add carry. Pairs with Entity_PosX_FracMid ($0588, attr 10), Entity_PosX_FracLo ($05a1, attr 11), and displayed integer Entity_X ($04c0, attr 2)
$0588: entity SOA attribute 10: middle byte of the 24-bit X position. The physics-tick at $b099-b09f does `lda $0397 / adc $0588,X / sta $0588,X` (adds X-velocity mid byte). Carries up into Entity_PosX_HiBit. Confirmed live: copied during slot-clone at $b1bf, also written at the bullet-init site $d080
$05a1: entity SOA attribute 11: lowest sub-pixel byte of the X position. The physics-tick at $b08f-b096 does `lda $0398 / clc / adc $05a1,X / sta $05a1,X` (adds X-velocity low byte). Carries into Entity_PosX_FracMid. Confirmed live: copied during slot-clone at $b1b9, cleared at the bullet-init site $d088
$c32d: zero-fill 18 of the 25 SOA attributes for slot X: writes #$00 to attrs 0 (Active), 1 (Y), 2 (X), 3 ($04d9), 5 (Direction), 6-11 (the 24-bit position fixed-point), 12 ($05ba), 14-18, and 21 ($069b). Notably skips attrs 4 ($04f2), 13 ($05d3 = enemy-type-ish), 19, 20, 22-24 — those are 'persistent' SOA fields that survive a slot reset. Caller responsibility: pass slot in X
$c320: loops X=3..24 calling EntityClear_Slot, wiping every non-protected entity slot. Saves and restores X via PHA/PLA. Slots 0-2 (player + 2 reserved) are intentionally untouched
$05d3: entity SOA attribute 13: per-slot enemy/entity type id. Read at $ee79 by EnemyType_DispatchInit which `asl A / tay / lda $eff2,Y / sta PPU_SrcLo / iny / lda $eff2,Y / sta PPU_SrcHi / jmp ($008a)` — i.e. uses the type as an index into the 16-bit pointer table EnemyType_InitDispatch ($eff2) and dispatches to the matching per-type init routine. Confirmed live: enemy_observe drift saw values 03 and 0a appear in slots 14/18/19/20 right after spawns; type 10 ($0a) maps to dispatch entry $ef49
$06cd: entity SOA attribute 23: per-slot raw enemy-type id as written by the spawn-script reader at $da3b-$da4d (which fills four consecutive slots Y..Y+3 from scratchpad bytes $b1/$b2/$b2/$b3 — a 4-enemy formation packet). Later copied into Entity_TypeId at $dadf when the slot is activated by Entity_ActivateFromSpawnTable. Survives EntityClear_Slot resets — that's why it's in the persistent-attrs band (4, 13, 19-24)
$eff2: 16-bit little-endian pointer table indexed by Entity_TypeId. Read at $ee7e/$ee84 by EnemyType_DispatchInit. First few entries: type 0 -> $ee8c (the canonical default-enemy init that sets attr 12=$11, attr 4=$02, attr 22=$44), type 1 -> $eeb4, type 2 -> $ee8c (alias of 0), type 3 -> $eed7, ... type 10 -> $ef49, types 11..21 mostly aliased to $ef6b. The aliasing pattern means this table maps logical enemy types (potentially many) to a smaller pool of init-handler implementations
$ee71: entity-activation tail: stores A into Entity_Active,X, then `lda #$ff / sta $069b,X` (mark attr 21), then reads Entity_TypeId, doubles it, and indirect-jumps via EnemyType_InitDispatch[type] (computed pointer staged in $008a:$008b). Each per-type init routine then sets the type-specific SOA fields (HP at attr 12, class at attr 4, $44-byte at attr 22, etc.)
$dad3: activates entity slot Y from the spawn-script staging area: saves Y to $071d, loads $071e as the spawn-table index, copies Entity_SpawnTypeRaw[$071e] to Entity_TypeId[Y] at $dadf, then `ora #$08` into Entity_Active[Y] (marks 'spawning' bit). The follow-on code (after $dae2) computes a sub-index from `(Y-13)*2` to look up additional per-spawn parameters
$05ba: entity SOA attribute 12: per-type init constant set by the EnemyType_InitDispatch handlers. Observed values: type 0 -> $11 ($ee93), type 1 -> $13 ($eeb6), type 3/4/7 -> $12 ($eed9/$eeec/$ef15). Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. Likely sprite tile-id base or HP/strength, but the per-type variation is small ($11..$14) so it's most consistent with a sprite/animation index
$06b4: entity SOA attribute 22: per-type init constant in the persistent SOA band. Observed values: type 0 -> $44 (set at $ee9d), type 1 -> $5c ($eec0), type 7 -> $48 ($ef1f), and $20 from the non-dispatch init at $b614 (player-bullet-style entity). Confirmed live: enemy_observe slot 21 took on $44 right after a wave spawned (type 0). Survives EntityClear_Slot — likely a per-spawn parameter the entity refers to throughout its life (animation timer reset, collision-box dimension, or score-value-id)
$04f2: entity SOA attribute 4: per-spawn class/category byte. Observed values across init paths: $00 (enemy types 3/4 at $eede/$eef0), $01 (b4bf/b626), $02 (default enemy and most types — $b5b4/$ee98/$eebb/$ef18), $03 (player bullet at $d092). Persistent across EntityClear_Slot. Functions as a small enum that downstream handlers branch on (movement style, collision behavior, etc.) — exact mapping not yet confirmed but the player-bullet vs enemy distinction at 3 vs 2 is the cleanest signal
$0682: entity SOA attribute 20: per-entity animation tick, advanced by +4 each animation step at $f590-$f596 (`lda $0682,X / clc / adc #$04 / sta $0682,X`) just before the animation loop reads from ($8a),Y. Initialized to $00 by several per-type inits ($ee02, $ef58, $ef82, $efe6) and to a passed value at $ed90. Cleared together with Entity_AnimFrame ($05ec, attr 14) at $ef85/$efe9. Persistent across EntityClear_Slot — survives slot reuse, which fits a 'where in the animation cycle did we leave off' role
$05ec: entity SOA attribute 14: paired with Entity_AnimTick ($0682, attr 20). Cleared together at $ef85/$efe9 by per-type inits, and zeroed by EntityClear_Slot. Likely the current animation frame index (consumer not yet pinned, but the lockstep clearing with the +4 animation tick is strong evidence)
$0605: entity SOA attribute 15: paired with the velocity-scratch zero-page byte $0396 in entity inits (e.g. $d646 stores the same constant 1 into both `$0396` and `$0605,X`). Also populated from the per-type velocity table at $fc53 by the loader at $f1ce-$f1d1. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. Most consistent reading: persistent X-velocity component (high bit), but the multi-purpose nature of $0396 keeps this label hedged
$061e: entity SOA attribute 16: paired with $0397 in the same $d646 / $fc53-loader pattern as Entity_Movement15. Constant 2 written to both $0397 and $061e,X at $d656. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. Likely persistent X-velocity mid byte
$0637: entity SOA attribute 17: paired with $0399 in $d64e (constant 0 to both). At $f1f5 it's set to `(($fc53,Y) - 1) & 1` — a single-bit flag. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. Likely a sign/direction bit for the Y velocity
$0650: entity SOA attribute 18: paired with $039a in $d65e (constant $d0 to both — note $d0 = -48 signed, the upward bullet velocity). Loaded from the per-type velocity table at $f1e9 with offset math relative to Entity_TypeParam22. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. Likely persistent Y-velocity mid byte
$069b: entity SOA attribute 21: per-entity update-tick counter. Initialized to $ff at every entity activation ($b4ba, $b5c8, $ee76 in EnemyType_DispatchInit) so the first INC wraps to 0. Each per-entity update INCs it ($b4e0, $b5da, $d1a5, etc.) and immediately reads the new value to drive periodic state changes: e.g. $b4e6 does `lsr A / and #$0f / cmp #$02` and stores a sprite tile to Entity_SpriteTile when the cycle is 0 or 1 (2-frame stretches in a 16-step animation), and $b5e0 does `and #$03 / bne / inc Entity_TypeParam12` to bump TypeParam12 every 4 ticks. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot
$04d9: entity SOA attribute 3: per-entity primary sprite tile-id. Written with computed or constant tile values throughout the codebase: $80/$81 in the FrameTick-driven 2-frame animation at $b4f0, $8e/$8f around the collision check at $b4c7/$b4d1, $10 at $b5c3, $79 at $b621, $20 at $b614. Cleared by EntityClear_Slot. With 41 distinct writers (highest of any SOA attr), this is the most-touched per-entity field — consistent with 'the sprite tile that gets DMA'd into OAM each frame'
$0669: entity SOA attribute 19: direction-derived flag. Set to 1 at $f25c when (Entity_Direction & $0f) < $08 — i.e. when the direction code is in the low half (UP, UP+RIGHT, RIGHT, DOWN+RIGHT — the right-or-neutral facings). The branch path at $f26a handles the high half (DOWN, DOWN+LEFT, LEFT, UP+LEFT — left-facing). Persistent across EntityClear_Slot. Most consistent reading: a sprite-flip / facing-mirror flag, but the consumer hasn't been pinned
$f880: enemy-death state machine. Reads Entity_TypeId and dispatches to one of three death animations: types >= $30 -> $f9ab (3-frame anim with sprite tiles $72..$74, FrameTick>>2 cycle), types $20..$2f -> $f955 (10-frame anim with sprite tiles $84..$8d, FrameTick>>1 cycle), types < $20 -> $f88a (4-frame anim with sprite tiles $80..$83, FrameTick cycle). After the animation, if Entity_Active bit $10 is set the routine awards score via Score_AddBCD then runs the per-type post-death effect at $f8e0 (DeathSprite_TypeMap → DeathSprite_TileClassTable). The handler ultimately deactivates the slot and calls EntityClear_Slot
$f8e0: maps Entity_TypeId to a 0..4 group index, used to look up a (sprite-tile, class) pair in DeathSprite_TileClassTable ($fcd3). Mapping: type $0a -> 0, type $0b -> 1, types $0c..$10 -> 2, types $11..$13 -> 3, all others -> 4. Result is doubled (asl A) and tya'd into Y, then `lda $fcd3,Y -> Entity_SpriteTile` and `lda $fcd3,Y+1 -> Entity_Class`. Encodes the post-explosion sprite + class for each enemy family
$fcd3: 5x2-byte (sprite-tile, Entity_Class) pairs consumed by DeathSprite_TypeMap. Bytes: 7a 02, 7b 01, 7c 03, 7e 00, 7d 01 — i.e. group 0 (type $0a) -> tile $7a class $02, group 1 (type $0b) -> tile $7b class $01, group 2 (types $0c..$10) -> tile $7c class $03, group 3 (types $11..$13) -> tile $7e class $00, group 4 (default) -> tile $7d class $01. The Entity_Class values written here ($00..$03) match the same enum used at activation time — hinting that the death residue is treated as a particular kind of inert entity until it fades
$f961: alternate death animation arm for enemy types $20..$2f. INCs Entity_FrameTick, then `lsr A / cmp #$0a` — runs 10 sprite frames (FrameTick 0..19, halved to 0..9), each frame writing tile ($84 + n) to Entity_SpriteTile. Calls L_f7cd (likely 'still on screen' check) and L_c275 (likely 'render'). Falls through to $f97d after 10 frames
$f9ab: alternate death animation arm for enemy types >= $30 (boss-class enemies). Queues PPU refresh #$12, then INCs Entity_FrameTick and uses (FrameTick >> 2) as a 3-frame counter. Each frame writes tile ($72 + n) to Entity_SpriteTile. After 3 frames falls into a tail (.byte block at $f9d4) that resets FrameTick to $ff and Entity_Class to $01 — restarts the death cycle once (boss enemies explode in two stages)
$f9e1: tail helper called at $f8dd (mid-anim 1) and $f939 (mid-anim 2): saves A to $b0, then `clc / adc #$b0 / sta Entity_SpriteTile,X / lda #$03 / sta Entity_Class,X`. Sets the post-explosion residue tile (relative to base $b0) and marks the slot as Entity_Class $03 ('inert death residue'). Also checks Entity_Active bit $40 to optionally trigger an extra-spawn at $fa07
$aa61: axis-aligned bounding-box overlap test between entity slot X (the 'attacker') and entity slot Y (the 'target'). Saves Y to $071d, copies Entity_X[X]/Entity_Y[X] to $0391/$0394 and Entity_X[Y]/Entity_Y[Y] to $0397/$039a, then twice dispatches via Hitbox_Switch on Entity_SpriteTile to fill $b0..$b3 with X's hitbox (left, right, top, bottom extents) and $b4..$b7 with Y's. The four-axis comparison at $aaa3-$ab1e tests whether the boxes overlap on every axis; on hit returns sec/rts (via $ab27), on miss clc/rts (via $ab22-ab26). Heavily called: 12+ sites including the player-bullet→enemy checks at $b56f/$b5ee/$b5f5/$b5fc, the bullet-hit-enemy chain at $f810-$f81e, and the enemy-extra-spawn collision at $f910/$f917/$f91e
$ab2c: switch on A (Entity_SpriteTile) → small integer hitbox-class index (0..15), then `jmp Hitbox_Lookup` to convert that index into a 4-byte hitbox pointer in $8a:$8b. The .byte block is a hand-rolled cascade of `cmp #tile / bne / lda #idx / jmp Hitbox_Lookup` arms covering individual sprite tiles ($00..$0f exact match) and tile ranges ($10..$5f → idx $0b, $60..$71 → idx $0b, $72..$78 → idx $0c, $79..$8d → idx $0d, $8e → idx $0e, $8f → idx $0f, default $10). The range arms ($ab8f-$abbf) handle the big sprite-tile families: enemy planes ($60..), explosion residue ($72-$78 from DeathSprite_TileClassTable), etc.
$abd0: 16 collision-box entries x 4 bytes each = 64 bytes total ($abd0..$ac0f), indexed by hitbox class via Hitbox_Lookup. Each entry is (left_extent, right_extent, top_extent, bottom_extent) in pixels, used by Collision_AABB to widen Entity_X/Y into a bbox. Sample entries: idx 0 = (5,5,4,3) small, idx 1 = (3,3,3,3) tiny symmetric, idx 4 = (17,17,5,5) wide+thin (boss-class plane?), idx 10 = (2,2,2,2) micro (bullet?), idx 13 = (18,18,10,10) largest. Note: byte $ac07 is shared between this table (last byte of idx 13) and L_ac07's `asl A` opcode — the disassembler sees $ac07 as a branch target but the byte is data here
$c275: entity fiber yield primitive. The whole entity system is a cooperative-multitasking fiber model: each entity runs as a coroutine on its own stack and calls this routine to suspend itself and let other entities (and HUD/input) run. Saves processor flags + A on the fiber's stack (php/pha), saves the fiber's X register at $42,X[$00], its Y register at $43,X[$00], and its current SP at $03,X[$00] — where $00 holds the slot index. Then jmps to Main_Loop to dispatch the next ready fiber. Called 60+ times throughout the codebase, mostly at the end of each per-entity frame's worth of work
$c288: yield variant that ALSO clears bit 0 of the fiber's status byte ($02,X) before suspending. Used by fibers that need to atomically transition out of a 'phase 1 active' state. The .byte block at $c29b is the actual `lda $02,X / and #$fe / sta $02,X / jmp Main_Loop` (rendered as bytes because the disassembler can't tell the .byte block from real code). Same save/dispatch protocol as Coroutine_Yield
$c2a4: yield variant that ALSO sets bit 0 of the fiber's status byte ($02,X) before suspending. Symmetric counterpart to Coroutine_Yield_ClearBit0. The .byte block at $c2b7 is `lda $02,X / ora #$01 / sta $02,X / jmp Main_Loop`. Used by fibers entering a 'phase 1 active' state
$c2f5: private continuation of Main_Loop after finding a ready fiber. Stores X (the matched slot index) back into Coroutine_ScanPos ($00), loads the fiber's saved SP from $03,X and `txs` to switch stacks, then restores the fiber's Y from $43,X, X from $42,X, and finally `pla / plp / rts` returns into the fiber's previously-saved code path. The rts pops a return address from the fiber's now-restored stack — that's how control jumps back into the fiber wherever Coroutine_Yield was called from
$0000: Main_Loop's current position when scanning the 16-slot fiber table. Strides by 2 (each slot is 2 bytes: status at $02,X, saved SP at $03,X). Reset to 0 when the scan reaches slot $20 (end of table) and a VBlank wait completes. Persists between scans within a frame — so once a fiber yields, the scheduler resumes scanning from the slot after it (round-robin scheduling, not always-from-zero)
$0002: per-fiber status byte (array of 16, indexed by Coroutine_ScanPos with stride 2: $02, $04, ..., $20). Bit 7 = 'ready to run' — Main_Loop only resumes fibers with this bit set. Bit 0 = a programmer-controllable phase flag, manipulated by Coroutine_Yield_ClearBit0 ($c288) and Coroutine_Yield_SetBit0 ($c2a4). Other bits unused (so far). Note: this is a fiber-table status, distinct from Entity_Active ($048e) which is the per-entity-slot SOA flag
$0003: per-fiber saved stack pointer (array of 16, $03/$05/.../$21). Each fiber owns a region of the 6502 stack page; this byte records where its SP was last left. On Coroutine_Yield: `tsx / sta $03,X[$00]`. On Coroutine_Resume: `lda $03,X / tax / txs`. The fact that each fiber has its own saved SP is what makes them true coroutines — they can yield from arbitrarily deep call chains and resume there
$0042: per-fiber saved X register (array of 16, $42/$44/.../$60). Saved by Coroutine_Yield via `txa / ldx $00 / sta $42,X`. Restored by Coroutine_Resume via `lda $42,X / tax`
$0043: per-fiber saved Y register (array of 16, $43/$45/.../$61). Saved by Coroutine_Yield via `tya / sta $43,X`. Restored by Coroutine_Resume via `lda $43,X / tay`
$c1f5: start a new fiber. Caller passes the slot index in A (must be even — stride 2 across the 16 fiber slots). If slot is already busy ($02,X bit 7 set), bails to Coroutine_Spawn_Tail without doing anything. Otherwise marks the slot ready, then constructs a 3-byte initial-stack frame in the slot's dedicated stack region: writes $04 (P-flags init), then the 2-byte (entry_point - 1) value from Fiber_EntryPointTable[slot] at $c3aa,X / $c3ab,X. Records the resulting SP (stack-base from $c38a,X minus 4) into Coroutine_SavedSP. The fiber actually starts executing the next time Main_Loop scans to it. Used at $c020/$c025 (game-init spawns slots 6 and 2), $b6bd (title screen), $b779/$b83c (menu transitions), and many more
$c230: private cleanup tail of Coroutine_Spawn: `pla / tay / pla / tax / pla / plp / rts` to restore caller's Y, X, A, and processor flags. Also reused as the bail target by Coroutine_Yield_ClearBit0 / SetBit0 / Kill_BySlot for their no-op early exit
$c237: exit current fiber: `ldx Coroutine_ScanPos / lda #$00 / sta Coroutine_Status,X / jmp Main_Loop`. Clears the running fiber's ready+phase bits and dispatches the next ready slot. The current fiber's saved SP and registers are now orphaned but harmless — Coroutine_Spawn will overwrite them when the slot is reused. Rendered as .byte block by the disassembler
$c240: exit a different fiber by slot index (passed in A). Same effect as Coroutine_Kill_Self but targeted: clears Coroutine_Status[A] and falls through to Coroutine_Spawn_Tail. Useful for one fiber to terminate another (e.g. a manager fiber dropping its workers)
$c251: yield variant that ALSO stamps a 16-bit callback/wakeup pointer from $86:$87 into the per-fiber Coroutine_Callback array ($22,X / $23,X), and sets bit 6 ($40) on Coroutine_Status[X]. Bit 6 marks 'fiber waiting on callback' — Main_Loop only resumes fibers with bit 7 set, so this combination ($c0 = bit 7 + bit 6) means 'ready AND waiting'. Some external event must clear bit 6 (or set bit 7) when the wait is satisfied
$0022: per-fiber 16-bit callback/wakeup pointer (array of 16, $22:$23 / $24:$25 / ... / $40:$41 — paired bytes, X stride 2). Stamped by Coroutine_YieldWithCallback ($c251) from $86:$87. Likely a function pointer the scheduler dispatches when waking the fiber, or an event identifier — exact consumer not yet pinned
$c38a: 16-byte table (one byte per fiber slot, X stride 2 — actually 32 bytes spanning $c38a..$c3a9 with the high-byte slot interleaved as $00 padding). Each entry is the top-of-stack offset within stack page $0100 for that slot. Sample values: slot 0 = $00, slot 1 = $0b, slot 2 = $1b, slot 3 = $2b, ..., slot 15 = $eb. Differences are 11..16 bytes per slot — that's the stack budget for each fiber. Read by Coroutine_Spawn at $c20b to compute the initial SP
$c3aa: 16-entry table of 16-bit (entry_point - 1) values, one per fiber slot. Read by Coroutine_Spawn at $c21e/$c224 (low/high bytes via $c3aa,X / $c3ab,X with X stride 2) and pushed onto the new fiber's stack so the eventual rts jumps to entry_point. Pointers (after +1): slot 0 = unused ($0000), slot 1 = $b65f Fiber_01_TitleScreen, slot 2 = $b7c3 Fiber_02_GameplaySession, slot 3 = $c3ca Fiber_03_BuildOAM, slot 4 = $d06a Fiber_04_PlayerControl, slot 5 = $ec0c, slot 6 = $ec45, slot 7 = $ec79, slot 8 = $ecad, slot 9 = $ecf1, slot 10 = $ed2a, slot 11 = $ed39, slot 12 = $ed48, slot 13 = $b3d5, slot 14 = $b509, slot 15 = $b588. Slots 5-12 cluster in $ec.. / $ed.. and from initial fiber_trace.py walks reach enemy-update / collision / score code (i.e. they are entity-update fibers, NOT the HUD/input cluster I initially guessed). Slots 13-15 are in the entity-update region $b3xx-$b5xx
$b65f: title-screen / menu fiber (slot 1). 420 reachable instructions; spawns slot 13 (entity manager, arg #$1a at $b6bd) early, then sits in TitleMenu_Handler ($b6c5) consuming Joypad1_State, MenuCursor_Index, Select_Debounce. On START, $b779 spawns slot 2 (Fiber_02_GameplaySession, arg #$04) and immediately kills self at $b77c. Yields once via Coroutine_Yield ($c275). Reads MenuCursor_Index, MenuCursor_Latched, TwoPlayer_Mode, GameState_Active; writes the menu cursor sprite, TitleMenu_FrameTimer, etc. Confirmed via fiber_trace.py --slot 1
$b7c3: gameplay-session fiber (slot 2). 1533 reachable instructions, the broadest fiber by far. Drives the per-life loop: NewGame_Init_P1, NewGame_Init_P2, P1_LifeLost / P2_LifeLost, Continue_Init_P1, SwapTo_P1 / SwapTo_P2, Death_Sequence, Reset_Rolls. Manages score additions (Score_AddBCD, Score_PostAdd, Score_Sync, Award_Extra_Life), HUD score-to-OAM (HUD_Score_To_OAM), and PPU refreshes (PPU_Queue_Refresh, PPU_WriteBlock). Yields 11 times, spawns 5 child fibers, kill-self twice (game-over paths). Started by Fiber_01_TitleScreen at $b779; effectively the game's per-game-session driver
$c3ca: per-frame OAM/HUD render fiber (slot 3). 191 reachable instructions, no spawns/kills, yields once at the bottom and loops back to top via the .byte block at $c454 (`jsr Coroutine_Yield / jmp $c3ca`). First half $c3ca-$c3f8: walk Entity_Active for 25 slots, copy live entities' (Y, SpriteTile, Class, X) quads to Player_OAM_Y ($03af) until 29 quads filled (cpy #$74 at $c3ef). Second half $c3f8-$c44e: pad remaining OAM Y-bytes with $ff (sprite-hide), then update HUD nametable tiles via $88/$8a indirect-page pointers (driven off $078c which is read at $c40e). Tightly contained — owns no game state, just renders other fibers' state into the shadow OAM and HUD tile queue every frame
$d06a: player input/movement fiber (slot 4). 1209 reachable instructions, 15 yields, no spawns. Drives the active player's per-frame logic: DPad_Handler ($d472), ApplyMovement ($d589), SetEntityDirection ($d593), Fire_Handler ($d2aa) → Spawn_Bullet ($d3b9), Roll_Handler ($d214) / Roll_Attempt ($d303) / Roll_Decrement_Site ($d309). Also reaches Collision_AABB at $aa61 and PPU_Queue_Refresh ($a06b). One kill-self (probably the game-over path that ends the session). Reads from the entity SOA + Joypad1_State; writes player Entity_X/Y/SpriteTile and Bullet_TargetSlot via Spawn_Bullet
$b3d5: player-bullet collision fiber (slot 13). 513 insns, 2 yields, no spawns/kills. Entry: ldx #$0b (player bullet slot, $0b=11). If $0380 == $ff (no bullet alive), runs the inline 'spawn placeholder bullet' arm at $b4f9 which sets Entity_Active[11] = $88 and SpriteTile = $01, then yields forever. Otherwise scans bullet slots 11..13 for active (`bmi`); for each found bullet, walks enemy slots 14..23 (ldy #$0e ... cpy #$18) calling Collision_AABB. On hit, calls Enemy_Death_Handler / increments Entity_AnimFrame and clears the bullet (Entity_Active &= $7f). NOT a generic 'collision fiber' — it specifically owns the P1 bullet group. Touches Collision_Player_JSR ($b44b), Hitbox_Switch, Hitbox_Lookup. Spawned by Fiber_01_TitleScreen at $b6bd with arg #$1a (slot 13)
$b509: second bullet/projectile collision fiber (slot 14). 434 insns, 1 yield, 0 kills. Entry: ldx #$03; scans entity slots 3..10 (cpx #$0b) for active (`bmi`). On match runs the same JSR $b048 / $b1d7 / $b37b movement+collision sequence used by slot 13, then a player-overlap test against slots 0..14 with masks #$21 and #$08. Best guess: drives the P2-bullet group and/or the lower entity-slot range that the player can shoot. Whether 'P2 bullets' or 'enemy-bullet/falling-debris collision' isn't yet pinned — both readings fit since the loop also tests `Entity_Active & $08` (sprite-flag) on the player slot to decide whether to apply damage
$b588: top-of-screen / boss-or-formation enemy driver fiber (slot 15). 1556 insns (largest after slot 2), 11 yields, 3 kill-self. Entry: ldx #$18 (entity slot 24, the topmost SOA slot). Sets Entity_Active[24]=#$0a, then reads Spawn_PatternIndex ($071f) and uses (idx & 1) << 2 to pick a 4-byte (PosX_HiBit, PosX_FracMid, PosY_HiBit, PosY_FracMid) record from the table at $b657. Sets Class=2, TypeParam12=1, Direction=0, SpriteTile=#$10, then yields and polls Entity_TickUpdate ($f7cd) until it returns nonzero. After spawn, runs collision against slots 0/1/2 (player) with three Collision_AABB calls; on hit jmp $f83c. Includes a TypeId=2 / Class=1 / SpriteTile=#$79 secondary phase at $b612 calling $f19c (per-type init) and Award_Extra_Life. Plausibly the boss / formation-leader controller — owns slot 24, runs the spawn-script, uses Score and Award_Extra_Life when the player kills it
$f7cd: run one tick of update logic for entity X and report alive/dead. Sequence: (1) if Respawn_Count == $ff branch to scoring/exit ($f83c), (2) load Entity_TypeParam12,X into A and JSR the standard motion/anim/cleanup trio at $b048, $b1d7, $b37b, (3) if Entity_Active & $08 (spawning bit) then skip the kill check, else if Entity_TypeId != $ff zero Entity_Active and JSR Coroutine_Kill_Self, (4) return with Z = (Entity_Active & $80 == 0). Called by Fiber_13/14/15 inside their per-bullet/per-enemy loops, by per-type animation reader $f570 at $f623, and by many other update sites. This is the canonical 'advance one entity by one frame' primitive — the closest thing the engine has to a per-frame-update dispatcher (it's Class-driven, not TypeId-driven)
$f6d1: look up the per-type animation/movement stream pointer for entity X and stage it in $8a:$8b. Sequence: lda Entity_TypeId,X / sec / sbc #$0a / asl A / tay / lda EnemyType_AnimTable,Y / sta $8a / iny / lda EnemyType_AnimTable,Y / sta $8b / ldy Entity_AnimTick,X / rts. Output: $8a:$8b = base of this entity's per-type stream, Y = current byte offset within the stream. Indexed by (TypeId - $0a), so it serves only TypeId values >= $0a (the player's bullets and the smallest enemies probably use a different path). Consumed by the (8a),Y readers at $f578-$f5ad (loads PosX/PosY/Movement bytes), $f5bf-$f5d8 (continuation), and the lda ($8a),Y sites at $f718, $f7xx, etc. — i.e. every site that reads per-type behavior bytes. Entry is $f6d1, NOT $f6d8 — the Phase 4 plan note about 'eleven unexplained lda ($8a),Y sites' resolves to this single setup routine plus its consumers
$fd4a: 16-bit little-endian pointer table indexed by (Entity_TypeId - $0a) * 2. 32 bytes spanning $fd4a..$fd69, holds 16 entries. Sample pointers: type $0a -> $fd9a, type $0b -> $fe2f, type $0c -> $fe50, type $0d -> $fe61, type $0e -> $fe72, type $0f -> $fe83, type $10 -> $fe94, type $11 -> $fea5, type $12 -> $feb6, type $13 -> $fed7, type $14 -> $fef8, type $15 -> $ff09, type $16 -> $ff2a, types $17/$18/$19 all alias to $ff4b. Each entry points to a per-type movement/animation byte stream that $f570 reads in 8-byte records (4 position bytes then 4 movement bytes), advancing Entity_AnimTick by 4 per record
$f570: per-type animation step: load the next 8-byte record from the entity's per-type stream and apply it to position + movement attrs. Sets Entity_FrameTick = $ff, calls EnemyType_GetAnimPtr to set up ($8a),Y, then reads 4 position bytes (PosX_HiBit, PosX_FracMid, PosY_HiBit, PosY_FracMid) at offsets 0..3, advances AnimTick by 4, reads 4 movement bytes (Movement15..18) at offsets 4..7, runs JSR $b102/$b0bc/$adc9 (motion helpers), then enters a Direction-update loop at $f5bc-$f622. Falls through to a final JSR Entity_TickUpdate at $f623. Effectively the 'play next frame of this enemy's pre-recorded movement script' routine
$ec0c: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $0e (= entity index 14). 1283 insns, 10 yields, 1 spawn, 2 kill-self. Entry: `ldx #$0e`, then check $078c / $078d (stage/scene mode flags). Branches to one of three formation-position arms (`$078c == $ff`, `== $80`, or default → jmp $ee5f the per-type update dispatch). Each arm hard-codes a starting (Entity_X, Entity_Y) for its variant (e.g. arm at $ec25 sets X=$70, Y=$48). Unique within the cluster: 1 spawn vs 0 for the other 7 — the spawn at $ec25 fires after `lda #$00 / sta $070d`, suggesting this slot kicks off a one-shot child fiber (effects? sub-formation?) before falling into the common $ed57 dispatcher
$ec45: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $0f. 897 insns, 7 yields, 2 kill-self. Same template as slot 5 but no spawn — pure positioner+dispatcher. Three arms based on $078c: arm $ec5f (X=$78, Y=$58), arm $ec6c (X=$68, Y=$78), default jmp $ee5f. Reaches enemy-death/collision/score code via the shared $ee5f update dispatcher
$ec79: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $10. 897 insns, 7 yields, 2 kill-self. Identical template to slot 6 with different position constants: arm $ec93 (X=$68, Y=$78), arm $eca0 (X=$88, Y=$88)
$ecad: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $11. 903 insns, 7 yields, 2 kill-self. Same template; arm $ecc7 sets X=$60, Y=$88, then conditional `inc Entity_SpriteTile,X` twice if Active_Player_Index != 0 (sprite-tile offset for P2 mode). Arm $ecdf sets X=$58, Y=$a8, Class=#$04 then jmp $ed5c (alternate dispatch entry)
$ecf1: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $12. 899 insns, 7 yields, 2 kill-self. Same template; arm $ed0b sets X=$18, Y=$d0, Class=#$03 then jmp $ed5c. Arm $ed1d sets X=$a0, Y=$b8 then jmp $ed57
$ed2a: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $13. 876 insns, 6 yields, 2 kill-self. Reduced template — only checks $078d (not $078c), so just two arms: matched (jmp $ee36) and default (jmp $ee5f main update dispatch)
$ed39: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $14. 876 insns, 6 yields, 2 kill-self. Identical reduced template to slot 10 but for the next entity slot
$ed48: formation-enemy fiber for entity slot $15. 876 insns, 6 yields, 2 kill-self. Identical reduced template to slot 10/11 for the last entity slot in the formation group. Note: slots 10-12 share the same code shape; the only static difference is the `ldx #$NN` value
